# Copilot Instructions — privacy-policies

## Repository Purpose

This repo generates per-app privacy policy HTML pages for Android apps published by Aleksandr Gladkov. Pages are hosted on GitHub Pages at `https://aleksandergladkov.github.io/privacy-policies/`.

The apps are built in a monorepo (`android-apps`) that shares core modules (`core-ads`, `core-analytics`, `core-billing`, `core-common`). Most apps have identical data-collection profiles; only permissions, extra SDKs, and on-device processing notes vary per app.

## Repo Structure

```
privacy-policies/
├── apps.yaml              ← per-app config (THE source of truth)
├── template.html           ← Jinja2 HTML template for policy pages
├── generate.py             ← Python script: reads apps.yaml + template.html → HTML files
├── index.html              ← GENERATED — do not hand-edit
├── bg-remover.html         ← GENERATED — do not hand-edit
├── swipe-pics.html         ← GENERATED — do not hand-edit
├── swipepics.html          ← LEGACY — hand-written, will be deleted after SwipePics review
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       └── deploy.yml      ← GitHub Actions: auto-generate + commit on push to main
└── .github/
    └── copilot-instructions.md  ← this file
```

## Critical Rules

1. **Never hand-edit generated HTML files.** Files like `bg-remover.html`, `swipe-pics.html`, and `index.html` are generated by `generate.py`. To change their content, edit `apps.yaml` or `template.html` and regenerate.
2. **Do not touch `swipepics.html`** — it is a legacy file still referenced by a live Play Store listing. It will be deleted later.
3. **Developer name is "Aleksandr Gladkov"** (not "Aleksander", not "Alexander").
4. **All HTML must be self-contained** — inline CSS only, no external stylesheets, no JavaScript, no external fonts. Pages are opened in mobile browsers from inside apps.

## How to Add a New App

Add an entry under `apps:` in `apps.yaml`:

```yaml
  app-slug:
    display_name: "App Display Name"
    description: "One-line description ending with a period."
    effective_date: "YYYY-MM-DD"
```

Optional fields (only add if non-default):

| Field | Default | When to set |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `uses_common_sdks` | `true` | Set `false` only if app has no ads/analytics/crashlytics |
| `has_billing` | `true` | Set `false` if app has no pro upgrade / IAP |
| `permissions` | `[]` | List `{name, reason}` pairs if app declares Android permissions |
| `extra_sdks` | `[]` | List `{name, data, purpose, policy_url}` for SDKs beyond the common set |
| `on_device_processing` | `[]` | Bullet points about what stays on device |
| `external_apis` | `[]` | List `{name, data, purpose, policy_url}` for third-party API calls |

Then run `python generate.py` to produce `<app-slug>.html` and update `index.html`.

## Common SDKs (shared by most apps)

These are defined in `apps.yaml` under `common_sdks` and appear on every page where `uses_common_sdks: true`:

- Google AdMob — ad serving
- Google UMP — GDPR consent
- Firebase Analytics — usage insights
- Firebase Crashlytics — crash reporting
- Google Play Billing — in-app purchases (conditional on `has_billing`)

## Tech Stack

- **Python 3** + **Jinja2** + **PyYAML** — the only dependencies
- **GitHub Actions** — auto-regenerates on push when `apps.yaml`, `template.html`, or `generate.py` change
- **GitHub Pages** — serves from `main` branch root

## Template Sections (in order)

1. Overview (app name, description, effective date)
2. Data Collection / Information We Collect (conditional on `uses_common_sdks`)
3. On-Device Processing (conditional on `on_device_processing`)
4. Permissions (conditional on `permissions`)
5. Third-Party Services with policy links (conditional on `uses_common_sdks` or `extra_sdks`)
6. External Services (conditional on `external_apis`)
7. Data Security + Your Rights / GDPR / CCPA (conditional on `uses_common_sdks`)
8. Children's Privacy
9. Changes to This Policy
10. Contact (developer email from config)

## URL Pattern

```
https://aleksandergladkov.github.io/privacy-policies/<app-slug>.html
```

This URL is used in:
- Each app's settings screen (`privacyPolicyUrl` parameter in `core-settings` `SettingsScreen`)
- Google Play Console privacy policy field
